items.title: Zahid says PAS’ call to reject Pakatan will only matter if it translates into votes for BN on July 11
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today welcomed any additional support for Barisan Nasional (BN) after PAS urged voters to reject Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, but said the only measure that mattered would be the outcome at the ballot box on July 11. Speaking to reporters after attending the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Kejora Charity Donation at Lotus Desaru in Kota Tinggi, Zahid said BN respected the positions taken by other political coalitions, including PAS’ recent call for voters to withhold support from PH. Asked about claims that the Johor election campaign had been subdued, Zahid dismissed the suggestion, saying BN’s campaign machinery remained active on the ground.

items.title: Quality listings key to market attractiveness: SC chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs to improve the quality of listed companies, strengthen corporate governance and encourage greater involvement by institutional investors to make its capital market more attractive to investors. "Good corporate governance alone does not beget investability," he said at the Institutional Investors Council Corporate Governance Conference 2026 here today. Mohammad Faiz said raising Malaysia's investability would require coordinated action across the capital market ecosystem under the Capital Market Masterplan 2026-2030 (CMP).

items.title: KJ: BN must engage Pas supporters to secure turnout at polls
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) machinery must actively engage with Pas voters, supporters and members in constituencies where the Islamist party is not contesting to ensure they turn out to vote for the coalition. Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said this was crucial amid concerns that Pas supporters might stay away from the polls despite directives from the party leadership to back BN candidates. We cannot assume that a statement from the Pas leadership means their voters will automatically support BN.

items.title: Seven in 10 Klang Valley adults overweight or obese, raising risks of heart, kidney and diabetes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Seven in 10 adults screened in the Klang Valley were found to be overweight or obese, underscoring Malaysia’s growing cardio renal metabolic (CRM) health crisis, according to the National Cancer Society Malaysia (NCSM) and Boehringer Ingelheim. Overall, 41.3 per cent were obese and 28.8 per cent overweight, while 34.5 per cent had pre diabetes and 35.1 per cent diabetes. The reports showed that 97.8 per cent of participants had at least one CRM risk factor, with nearly 68 per cent facing three or more.

items.title: Four decades on, Pahang Sultan recounts tragic Pekan school fire
items.summary: KUANTAN: Some four decades after a tragic school fire in Pekan, the Sultan of Pahang today recalled an incident that has remained etched in his memory. Speaking at the opening of the Sungai Lembing fire and rescue station, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said the tragic incident, which occurred in the early 1980s when he had just returned from abroad, still lingers in his mind to this day. "Speaking about the fire and rescue department, I feel compelled to share a story involving me and them during my younger days.

items.title: As Pengerang’s population grows, DPM Zahid backs hospital and district status plans
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 2 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has agreed to a proposal to build a hospital in Pengerang, near here, citing its growing population. Also present was Azalina, who is also minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), in her capacity as the Pengerang MP. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said the construction of a hospital and turning Pengerang into a new district were part of Kejora’s long-term plans in collaboration with the Johor state government.

items.title: Teacher claims trial to injuring 7-year-old pupil with whiteboard eraser
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 47-year-old female teacher pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to injuring a 7-year-old pupil by throwing a whiteboard eraser at her. The accused, Fakhriah Bustani, claimed trial after the charge under Section 323 of the Penal Code was read out before magistrate Mustaqim Sukarno. She was accused of intentionally causing injury to the pupil at a national school in Tongkang Pecah here, at 9am on April 1.

items.title: Johor election: Maszlee to campaign in all areas in Puteri Wangsa
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Puteri Wangsa, Dr Maszlee Malik, is not intimidated by campaigning in any polling district centre in the constituency, including areas considered difficult for PH to penetrate. Describing campaign time as the best opportunity to meet voters and present his message, he said PH has never categorised any polling district centre as either safe or difficult. "For PH, the people have dignity, face and self-respect.

items.title: DAP bets on new generation of candidates to shake up Johor election
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 2 — DAP is confident that its line-up of first-time candidates contesting under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner in the Johor state election can bring meaningful change to their respective constituencies. DAP deputy secretary-general Steven Sim Chee Keong said the Johor DAP leadership’s decision to field candidates who had never contested an election reflected the party’s commitment to giving younger leaders the opportunity to step forward and serve the people. Also present were DAP assistant national publicity secretary Young Syefura Othman and the party’s Parit Raja candidate Shazwan Dzainal Abidin.

items.title: Nearly 20 million QR clearances recorded at Johor CIQs, says Saifuddin
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Nearly 20 million QR code transactions have been recorded at Johor's two main land checkpoints since the rollout of the MyNIISe system, says Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. He said the digital initiative had helped ease congestion at the Malaysia-Singapore border. "As of Sunday (June 28), the MyNIISe application had recorded 2.4 million downloads and 1.27 million users, while 19.48 million QR code transactions were recorded at both the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complexes at Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB).

items.title: Johor polls: Muar district office denies barring Anwar from using govt complex
items.summary: MUAR: The Muar district office today denied claims that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was barred from using the Penghulu Mukim Sungai Balang Complex for a community engagement programme last month. Muar district officer Zainal Eran said the event was relocated following administrative concerns over an incomplete application and the risk of creating a political perception. "The district administration never intended to prevent the prime minister from meeting fishermen, farmers, entrepreneurs and educators during his visit on June 15."

items.title: Amirudin: Selangor economy surges to RM460.1b in 2025, contributing 26.5pc of Malaysia’s GDP
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 2 — Selangor’s economy expanded by RM28 billion in 2025 to reach RM460.1 billion, accounting for 26.5 per cent of Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP), Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. The expansion was driven by the services sector (RM15.9 billion), manufacturing (RM5.3 billion) and construction (RM3.7 billion). Selangor now contributes 35.9 per cent of Malaysia’s construction activity, 32.8 per cent of manufacturing and 27.1 per cent of services.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution says MyNIISe app hits 2.4 million downloads, 19.48 million QR scans easing Johor Causeway jams
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Home Ministry’s MyNIISe application has recorded more than 19.48 million QR transactions at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB, Johor) as of June 28, 2026, said Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. He said the app, which facilitates faster immigration clearance, has been downloaded 2.4 million times, with 1.27 million registered users. Saifuddin added that the initiative has also been extended to five major airports nationwide, where 5.59 million transactions have been recorded.

items.title: [UPDATED] Couple arrested over viral Federal Highway hit-and-run crash
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police arrested a married couple in connection with a three-vehicle crash on the Federal Highway here that went viral after the driver allegedly fled⁠ the scene. Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division detained the couple, both in their 50s, about 9pm yesterday over the June 20 incident involving a Perodua Kembara. The video, circulated widely on social media, triggered public outrage and calls for police action.

items.title: Navy chief: More than 60pct of fleet now over 40 years old
items.summary: Its chief Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain said the situation had become one of the navy's most pressing operational challenges, driven by rising maintenance costs, difficulty sourcing spare parts and concerns over long-term seaworthiness. He said older ships increasingly required corrective maintenance rather than preventive maintenance, resulting in higher costs and more frequent breakdowns. "Once ships become obsolete, corrective maintenance overtakes preventive maintenance.

items.title: Zahid backs proposal for Pengerang to become new district
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has voiced his support for a proposal to designate Pengerang as a new administrative district alongside Kota Tinggi. He said both proposals put forward by Pengerang member of parliament Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said should be given serious consideration as they would directly benefit the people. Pengerang district.

items.title: Musk to remain world's only dollar trillionaire for long time, says US expert
items.summary: Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin rank second and third, with their wealth rising by US$32.2 billion to US$301 billion and by US$29.9 billion to US$280 billion, respectively. Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is in fourth place with US$262 billion, up US$8.42 billion. Dell Technologies founder Michael Dell rounds out the top five with US$217 billion, up US$77.1 billion.

items.title: Johor election: Pas move not automatic BN advantage in Johor polls, says Zahid
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Pas' move urging its supporters not to vote for Pakatan Harapan in seats contested by the opposition coalition should not be seen as an automatic advantage for Barisan Nasional in the Johor election. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said BN would focus entirely on the strength and credibility of its candidates in the July 11 polls. "That is their (Pas) approach.

items.title: More than 2 million military casualties in Ukraine war: Study
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Russia's invasion of Ukraine has caused more than two million military casualties, with Moscow's forces bearing the brunt of the losses, according to a study published Wednesday by a US think tank. "Combined Russian and Ukrainian casualties have exceeded 2 million," the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) said. It estimated that between 400,000 and 450,000 Russians have been killed since Moscow invaded Ukraine in February 2022, out of a total of 1.4 million casualties – killed, wounded and missing – among Russian troops.

items.title: Government to kick off 18‑month National Gentleman Study to see if men can be wise, kind and tidy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM) will kick off the Kajian Lelaki Budiman Negara (National Gentleman Study) this month under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) to develop evidence-based policies and initiatives for men’s empowerment. Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the 18-month study would support the government’s National Gentleman Initiative, aimed at nurturing resilient, responsible men who can contribute meaningfully to family well-being and national development. Its findings and recommendations will provide key input for policymaking and programme development on men’s empowerment in Malaysia, including the implementation of the National Gentleman Study, she said.

items.title: Man rescued unharmed as fire guts 2 shophouses in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A man was rescued unharmed after a fire gutted two three-storey shophouses housing a restaurant in Jalan Perak here today. A team from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by personnel from the Lebuh Pantai station, arrived at the scene a minute after receiving the emergency call. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said 11 personnel, including supervisors, were deployed for the operation, supported by a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit, Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) and an Aerial Ladder Platform (ALP).

items.title: Saifuddin wins bid to uphold default judgment in defamation suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has succeeded in upholding a default judgment in his defamation suit against a social media user over posts linked to Kedah politics and Kedah Darul Aman FC. The Alor Star High Court dismissed Muhammad Zubair Ismail's application to set aside the default judgment after finding that he had failed to establish a defence warranting a trial. High Court Judge Dr Mohammad Johan Lee, in grounds of judgment dated June 30, ruled that the defendant had failed to file a statement of defence despite being served via substituted service.

items.title: SoftBank's LY, Bain raise Kakaku bid again with US$4.1bil valuation
items.summary: TOKYO: SoftBank's LY Corp and Bain Capital have again raised their bid for Kakaku.com , valuing the Japanese price-comparison website operator at 670 billion yen (US$4.12 billion) and widening the gap over a rival offer from Sweden's EQT. In the legally binding offer unveiled late on Wednesday, LY and Bain said they would raise their bid for all outstanding Kakaku.com shares to 3,384 yen apiece from 3,232 yen each proposed in May. LY and Bain said their offer would increase to 3,500 yen a share if KDDI Corp, one of Kakaku.com's largest shareholders, agreed to support the bid.

items.title: Liew: Malaysia must boost women's workforce participation to achieve high-income status
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must increase women's participation in the workforce if it hopes to accelerate income growth and achieve high-income nation status, Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong said today. Citing the latest figures released by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Liew said women now make up almost 60 per cent of Malaysia's civil service, with about 43 per cent holding leadership positions — among the highest rates globally for a Muslim-majority country. However, he said female labour force participation in the broader economy remained comparatively low, representing one of Malaysia's biggest untapped opportunities for economic growth.

items.title: Malaysia must grow economy to tackle household debt, says Liew
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must boost economic growth and incomes, alongside responsible lending, to tackle household debt, which stood at 84.4 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP) in March. Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong said household debt stood at RM1.73 trillion and had remained above 80 per cent for almost 15 years following the global financial crisis. "Malaysia needed to accelerate economic growth while ensuring wages increased alongside productivity.

items.title: Super Micro says two Taiwan staff detained in probe involving its AI servers
items.summary: TAIPEI: Super Micro said on Wednesday that two employees at its Taiwan unit were detained pending a court hearing, while two others were released on bail in a probe into alleged illegal exports of AI servers containing Nvidia chips. The servers are made by Super Micro and contain Nvidia chips, which are subject to US export controls prohibiting export to China. The companies searched were Super Micro Taiwan, Albatron Technology, Super Micro's distributor in Taiwan, and Chief Telecom, a data centre operator.

items.title: Domestic reform key to global contributions, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia must first put its own house in order by strengthening governance, stressing that the country's ability to make meaningful contributions on the global stage depends on successful domestic reform. Speaking during a question-and-answer session at the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable today, Anwar said his government's priority is to improve governance, eradicate corruption, reduce inequality, and ensure every citizen has access to quality education and healthcare. He said while Malaysia will continue taking principled positions on international issues, its immediate focus remains on building a better nation and demonstrating that a Muslim-majority country can uphold democratic values, moderation, and inclusivity.

items.title: Pahang Sultan calls for river dredging to strengthen flood mitigation
items.summary: KUANTAN: Building embankment walls without deepening riverbeds will be ineffective in preventing floods, the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, said. The ruler reiterated his call for shallow rivers to be deepened, noting that embankments would be of little use if rivers continued to silt up. Al-Sultan Abdullah expressed gratitude to the state and federal governments for allocating substantial funds for infrastructure in Pahang, especially the RM100 million Sungai Lembing flood mitigation project.

items.title: Dzulkefly reassures Dewan Rakyat RM500m savings due to vacancies, not frontline care cuts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The RM500 million expenditure restriction warrant imposed on the Ministry of Health (MOH) is a technical adjustment and does not affect the delivery of healthcare services to the public, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. He said the adjustment involved surplus allocation provided for positions that could not be filled, thereby enabling the government to save RM500 million without affecting the ministry’s operations. In this regard, he said claims that the adjustment would affect services at hospitals, especially in rural areas, were inaccurate as all basic services and health development projects would continue.

items.title: Hardware store worker charged with raping, sexually assaulting teenage sister
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A 25-year-old hardware store worker pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here to charges of raping and sexually assaulting his 16-year-old biological sister. The accused claimed trial after both charges were read out before judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli. Section 14(d) carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping, while Section 16(1) provides for an additional prison term of up to five years and a minimum of two strokes of the rotan.

items.title: Mat Sabu to represent Malaysia at Ali Khamenei's funeral in Teheran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu will represent Malaysia at the funeral of Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Teheran tomorrow. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he had been invited to attend the ceremony but was unable to do so due to prior commitments. "For the funeral of Khamenei tomorrow in Teheran, we are sending our agriculture minister to represent the country," he said during a fireside chat at the 37th Asia-Pacific Roundtable today.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang recalls tragic school fire that still haunts him
items.summary: Speaking at the opening of the Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said the tragedy in the early 1980s, shortly after his return from abroad, remained vivid to this day. "Speaking about the Fire and Rescue Department, I feel compelled to share a story from my younger days. I went to the scene and saw one block of the building on fire," he said.

items.title: Nancy: Ministry launches 18-month National Gentlemen Study to address mental health, social issues
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry will launch the National Gentlemen Study (Kajian Lelaki Budiman Negara) this month to develop policies and initiatives aimed at empowering men. Its minister, Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri, said the study under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) will span 18 months to help nurture resilient, responsible men who contribute significantly to national and family well-being. Nancy said this at the opening ceremony of the Men's Empowerment Consultative Forum at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre today.

items.title: Johor election: Bersatu members backing PN rivals committing sabotage, says Tun Faisal
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Bersatu has slammed party members who threw their support behind political parties contesting against Perikatan Nasional in the Johor election. He said Bersatu does not support PH or BN in the July 11 polls. "There is no decision by the PN Supreme Council, nor any official PN policy, instructing or allowing support for PH or BN.

items.title: Weak referral system leads to rising demand, cost pressures: Private hospitals body
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's healthcare system is facing a structural gap in primary care integration with private hospital stakeholders warning that general practitioners (GPs) are not being effectively used as gatekeepers, leading to unnecessary hospital utilisation. Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) honorary secretary Anwar Anis said the weak primary care referral system is contributing to rising demand and cost pressures across the healthcare sector. Anwar said the World Bank findings support the view that rising healthcare costs are driven mainly by higher utilisation rather than treatment price increases.

items.title: Three foreigners arrested, RM769,480 illicit cigarettes seized in Johor Baru raid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police seized illicit cigarettes worth RM769,480 and arrested three foreigners in a raid on a house in Taman Daya under Op Taring Alpha 1 on Wednesday night. WCB/PSK commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said two Bangladeshi men, aged 26 and 49, and a 51-year-old Indonesian woman were detained. He said police also seized 292,000 sticks of suspected duty-unpaid cigarettes of various brands from the house and a Proton Exora parked at the premises.

items.title: India tells WhatsApp to halt usernames rollout, justify feature or face action
items.summary: India has asked WhatsApp to justify the implementation of a planned feature covering usernames and to freeze the rollout in its biggest market, escalating a crackdown on messaging anonymity that began with Telegram, according to a government letter reviewed by Reuters. Earlier this week, Meta's WhatsApp said it had begun a phased global rollout, including in India, of the feature, which lets users reserve a unique username and eventually message others without sharing their phone numbers. Anonymity was among the concerns India cited when it temporarily blocked the messaging app Telegram last month.

items.title: Can one person build the next startup? Penang launches AI incubator for solo founders
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 2 — Penang today launched an AI-powered one-person company incubator programme under Digital Penang to nurture solo creative technopreneurs. State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the programme reflects the government’s push to encourage and facilitate the growth of digital startups, particularly by leveraging AI tools to enable leaner business models. The initiative, called the Creative Creator AI One-Person Company (AI OPC) Incubator Programme, is designed to support individuals in building and operating digital startups as solo founders using AI systems.

items.title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that his acting spy chief has wide permission to declassify records, including any tied to the 2020 election, even though his close ally is only at the intelligence helm for a short time. Trump appointed Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence last month, elevating a political loyalist with no national security experience at a time of war and global tensions. Following a political backlash over his pick, Trump subsequently nominated Jay Clayton, the top US attorney for Manhattan.

items.title: Flexi Parking and Smart Selangor Parking apps back online after cyberattack, but some users flag missing credit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — If you are a user of the Flexi Parking or Smart Selangor Parking apps, you can start using them to pay for parking again, as services have been restored following cyberattacks. Subsequently, both Flexi Parking and Smart Selangor Parking made similar Facebook announcements on how their app users can report issues that they continue to face. Recommended reading: Selangor confirms Flexi Parking cyberattack, suspends parking summons during outage

items.title: No one-size-fits-all approach to school safety, says education minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) will assess incidents involving student safety in schools individually, as each situation is different, said its minister Fadhlina Sidek. She said that at the same time, the ministry was strengthening audits and monitoring of school safety, including through the establishment of a special committee comprising various agencies and organisations to ensure all aspects of safety were given due attention and a safe school ecosystem could be created. “We have received full support and expertise from the National Institute of Occupational Safety and Health to ensure that matters related to buildings, drains and fire safety are given proper guidance, alongside training to enhance safety coordination at school level,” she said during Minister’s Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia must put its own house in order before changing the world
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia’s foremost responsibility is to strengthen governance at home, stressing that meaningful contributions to global affairs must begin with domestic reform. Speaking during a question-and-answer session at the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable here today, Anwar said his government’s priority was to improve governance, eradicate corruption, reduce inequality and ensure every citizen has access to quality education and healthcare. He said while Malaysia would continue taking principled positions on international issues, its immediate focus remained on building a better nation and demonstrating that a Muslim-majority country could uphold democratic values, moderation and inclusivity.

items.title: Johor polls: Bukit Batu voters’ election wish list starts with better jobs and ends with smoother roads
items.summary: KULAI, July 2 — Rising cost of living, better job opportunities and improved infrastructure are among the key concerns of residents in the Bukit Batu state constituency ahead of the 16th Johor state election. Taman Sri Pulai 1 resident Kelvin Chong, 58, said the state government, particularly the Bukit Batu elected representative, should focus on creating more quality job opportunities with competitive salaries to help the people cope with the rising cost of living. “People are hoping for more job opportunities with better salaries.

items.title: ‘Scammers are watching’: Govt forms new cross-agency anti-scam committee but keeps tactics under wraps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The government has established a special working committee and technical group to address online fraud or scam-related crimes on June 18, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi, who is also the Madani Government spokesman, said the cross-agency approach is aimed at strengthening enforcement, legal and investigative efforts to enhance user safety on digital platforms. Elaborating, Fahmi said the government expects several positive developments to be achieved in the near term following the establishment of the committee.

items.title: Existing Tanjung Sepat pig farms don’t need EIAs because they predate 2015 order, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Existing pig farms in Tanjung Sepat are not required to submit Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports as they were already operating before the 2015 EIA Order came into effect, the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry said today. Deputy Minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh said the EIA requirement applies only to the development of new pig farming areas involving herds of 2,000 pigs or more. “From the perspective of NRES, the preparation of the EIA report is only mandatory for the establishment of new pig farming areas involving a standing population of 2,000 pigs or more, as stipulated under the Environmental Quality (Prescribed Activities) (Environmental Impact Assessment) Order 2015.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia needs sovereign cloud to protect critical data while keeping doors open to Big Tech
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia needs to set up a sovereign cloud to safeguard critical security and personal data while remaining open to global digital investments as South-east Asia becomes increasingly dependent on critical digital technologies and infrastructure. Responding to a question on how Malaysia and South-east Asian countries could exercise agency in an increasingly interconnected digital landscape, he said the sovereign cloud would enable Malaysia to protect sensitive information while continuing to embrace openness in a globalised world. Anwar said the sovereign cloud would have firewalls to protect critical security and personal information while acknowledging there were limits to such protection in an increasingly globalised world.

items.title: Belgian deputy PM Prevot begins first Malaysia visit with focus on clean energy and trade
items.summary: SEPANG, July 2 — Belgium’s Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs, European Affairs and Development Cooperation, Maxime Prevot, arrived in Malaysia today for a two-day working visit, his first to the country since assuming office in February 2025. Also present was Malaysia’s chief of protocol Datuk Ikram Mohammad Ibrahim. Prevot’s visit is expected to further strengthen bilateral relations between Malaysia and Belgium.

items.title: Loke: Putrajaya to help fund Johor’s RM10b E-ART as private funding alone ‘not viable’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The federal government will provide funding for Johor’s Autonomous Rapid Transit (E-ART) project, while the initial phase will be financed by the appointed consortium, Transport Minister Anthony Loke told Parliament today. The project, estimated to cost RM10 billion, received Cabinet approval just last month. The E-ART is meant to complement the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link as a solution to the projected rise in traffic resulting from the state’s rapid development.

items.title: Smart Lane cuts highway congestion by up to 50pc, accident rates remain low, says Nanta
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The Smart Lane traffic management initiative has reduced congestion on selected highways by up to 50 per cent since its implementation, according to Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi. “The Smart Lane initiative has demonstrated strong effectiveness, reducing traffic congestion by between 30 per cent and 50 per cent. “In terms of safety, accident rates within Smart Lane zones are very low compared to overall traffic volume,” he told the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Over RM500m in micro-financing approved for 30,000 entrepreneurs in six weeks, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: He said the amount was part of the government’s allocation of more than RM5 billion under the Micro Financing Facility Programme to help micro enterprises sustain their businesses amid rising costs. The financing was disbursed through Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), Agrobank, Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN), Bank Rakyat, MARA and Tekun Nasional. “The government has also rolled out the RM5 billion Madani Government Assistance Guarantee Scheme through Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Bhd (SJPP) to ensure micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) have access to financing.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s Mahkota candidate pitches ‘work in the city, live in the countryside’ vision
items.summary: KLUANG, July 2 — A “Work in the City, Live in the Countryside” ecosystem is at the heart of Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Syed Hussien Syed Abdullah’s vision for Mahkota state constituency, aimed at enabling residents to secure better-paying jobs in Johor’s urban and industrial centres while continuing to enjoy a more affordable and sustainable lifestyle. He said the initiative would allow residents to benefit directly from Johor’s economic expansion through improved public transport connectivity, particularly the Electric Train Service (ETS), making daily commuting between Kluang and major employment hubs more convenient. Syed Hussien said the vision aligns with the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), introduced by Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, which seeks to spur balanced economic growth across all 10 districts in the state.

items.title: Police bust online World Cup betting ring, arrest 32 Chinese nationals in Cheras
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Police have dismantled an online football betting syndicate following the arrest of 32 Chinese nationals in a raid at an office unit in Cheras early yesterday morning. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the raid, carried out at about 2.40 am by the Criminal Investigation Division of the Cheras District Police Headquarters (IPD), resulted in the arrest of 29 men and three women aged 20 to 40. Fadil said the case was being investigated under Section 4(1)(c) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953, Section 4(1)(e) of the Betting Act 1953 and Section 6(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: Malaysia to send minister to Khamenei funeral in Tehran, says Anwar; Iran envoy confirms Mat Sabu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Malaysia will send a ministerial-level representative to attend the funeral of Iran’s supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran tomorrow, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The funeral arrangements, however, were delayed until July due to the ongoing war involving US-backed Israeli forces. Separately, Iranian ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi told Malay Mail that Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu would represent Malaysia at the funeral in Tehran tomorrow.

items.title: Check for bank logos: RM1 ATM fee waiver only applies to bank-owned machines, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today reminded the public that the RM1 interbank ATM withdrawal fee exemption introduced yesterday only applies to ATMs owned and operated by banks, and not those run by independent operators under separate commercial agreements. “The exemption applies to bank-owned ATMs, which make up 84 per cent, or nearly 16,000 ATMs, nationwide. “At present, all ATMs owned by banks have already waived the RM1 fee.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Malaysia to turn retiring coal plants into renewable energy hubs under new plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Malaysia plans to repurpose sites of retiring coal-fired power plants into renewable energy (RE) hubs and battery energy storage facilities under a proposed National Coal Site Repurposing Framework, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said the country’s existing coal fleet represented significant national infrastructure, including transmission connections, industrial facilities and strategically located land. “Selected sites possess strong potential to be transformed into renewable energy hubs, battery energy storage facilities and other clean energy developments that continue contributing to local economies while supporting our national transition objectives,” he said.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia will stay non-aligned but never neutral on Palestine, sovereignty or Islamophobia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Malaysia will maintain an independent and non-aligned foreign policy but will not remain neutral on the Palestinian cause, Islamophobia or matters involving national sovereignty. Anwar said the country will not shy away from taking a principled stance, even on issues involving powerful partners such as the United States (US) and China. “First and foremost, Malaysia’s foreign policy will remain independent, and that is fiercely independent, proactive and non-aligned.

items.title: Fahmi: Putrajaya exploring agentic AI after MCMC’s harmful content workload hits equivalent of 19.7 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government is exploring the use of agentic artificial intelligence (AI) as the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) grapples with a growing workload in online safety enforcement. He said the scale of enforcement has surged significantly, with MCMC identifying and requesting the removal of 345,712 pieces of harmful online content since January 1. Therefore, Fahmi said both sides must use AI, with platforms deploying AI systems while MCMC also adopts AI to manage harmful online content.

items.title: SPM candidate who says he takes ketum to stay awake to study among 195 detained in Negeri Sembilan drug raids
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 2 — A total of 195 individuals, including a Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) candidate, tested positive for drugs and were detained by the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) through the three-day Ops Perdana that was conducted simultaneously in Seremban, Jempol, Tampin and Port Dickson yesterday. AADK deputy director-general (Operations) Zainudin Abdullah said the operation, which began on June 29, involved 72 Negeri Sembilan AADK personnel. “We also detained a married couple, aged 45 and 44, who tested positive for methamphetamine and ketum.

items.title: Norway’s missile decision has not hurt Malaysia-EU ties, Anwar says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Leaders from the European Union (EU) have reaffirmed their support for Malaysia after Norway revoked naval strike missile (NSM) exports to the country, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. In response, he said he received letters from French President Emmanuel Macron, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Germany’s leaders stating that they fully supported Malaysia on the matter. Anwar said he was also puzzled by the “security considerations” cited by Norway in abruptly cancelling the deal after Malaysia had nearly settled the payments in full.

items.title: Khaled: LCS project stays on track, Malaysia seeks replacement for scrapped Norwegian missiles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Malaysia’s Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) programme will remain on schedule despite Norway’s decision to revoke the export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system, with the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) evaluating replacement missiles as the government seeks to recover RM571.9 million already paid under the affected procurement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the revocation would not affect the LCS delivery schedule, while the Defence Ministry assesses alternative missile systems for the fleet. He said the ministry was extending the service life of existing assets, diversifying defence procurement sources and strengthening defence diplomacy to ensure strategic supply chains remained intact.
